source_name: Australian Defence uncrewed systems and counter-drone public posture
notes: Manual public-source uncrewed systems and counter-drone profile for the Australian Defence posture page. Rows are kept conceptually separate by capability_type so introduced, acquisition, development, sovereign program, and counter-drone categories are not collapsed. Quantities cite the named Defence project URL rather than asserting a specific operational fleet number; readiness, mission-capable rates, deployability and a clean drone-only budget remain unavailable.
source_rights: Commonwealth copyright; Defence copyright terms apply
citation: Department of Defence project pages (AIR 7000 Phase 1B Triton; AIR 6014 Phase 1 MQ-28A Ghost Bat; LAND 129 Phase 3 Integrator; LAND 156 counter-small UAS); Australian Submarine Agency / Department of Defence Ghost Shark public announcements; Sovereign Defence Industrial Priorities (SDIP) - Autonomous Systems; 2024 Integrated Investment Program (IIP) and 2026 National Defence Strategy budget factsheet.

fields.section_intro: Selected publicly stated rows for Australia's uncrewed-systems posture. The page keeps in-service / acquisition / development / sovereign program / counter-drone rows separate. Quantities are described as the public source describes them; this section does not publish a complete ADF drone inventory.
fields.boundary: No complete ADF uncrewed inventory is published here. No readiness, mission-capable rates, deployability or operational-availability values are loaded. The 2024 Integrated Investment Program references uncrewed and autonomous-system lines, but does not publish a clean drone-only budget; that figure is left unavailable.

systems_rows.quantity_or_status: Acquisition delivered against Project AIR 7000 Phase 1B; first aircraft transferred to RAAF and progressively introduced into service.
systems_rows.operator_or_program: Royal Australian Air Force (Project AIR 7000 Phase 1B)
systems_rows.notes: Long-range maritime ISR UAS; quantity per the named public Defence project page rather than a separately published operational fleet count.

systems_rows.quantity_or_status: Sovereign-developed under Project AIR 6014 Phase 1 with Boeing Defence Australia; prototype aircraft built and flying. Not in operational service.
systems_rows.operator_or_program: Royal Australian Air Force test and evaluation (Project AIR 6014 Phase 1)
systems_rows.notes: Australian-designed and -built uncrewed aircraft. Treat as a development program; not a fielded combat capability.

systems_rows.quantity_or_status: In service with the Australian Army under Project LAND 129 Phase 3 since the program was acquired; numbers not separately published in the named project page.
systems_rows.notes: Tactical fixed-wing UAS for Army artillery / surveillance and target acquisition use; small, runway-independent, catapult-launched.

systems_rows.quantity_or_status: Project LAND 156 establishes a layered Army counter-small-UAS capability. Acquisition is in progress; published material does not list a final fleet count or radar/effector mix.
systems_rows.notes: Counter-drone capability is treated as a separate row from uncrewed systems Australia operates; it defends against adversary drones rather than being one.

systems_rows.system_name: Ghost Shark Extra-Large Autonomous Undersea Vehicle
systems_rows.quantity_or_status: Co-funded sovereign development with Anduril Australia. Prototype build publicly announced; not in operational service.
systems_rows.operator_or_program: Royal Australian Navy / Department of Defence (Anduril Australia)
systems_rows.notes: Sovereign-developed XL-AUV. Operational availability and crewless-deployment capacity are not published.

systems_rows.system_name: Sovereign Defence Industrial Priorities - autonomous systems
systems_rows.quantity_or_status: Autonomous systems is one of the published Sovereign Defence Industrial Priorities; this is a stated industrial-capability category, not a fielded capability count.
systems_rows.operator_or_program: Department of Defence - Sovereign Defence Industrial Priorities (SDIP)
systems_rows.notes: Industrial priority context only. Not equivalent to readiness or fleet counts.

systems_rows.quantity_or_status: The Royal Australian Navy publicly references maritime autonomous systems in its Warfare Innovation and uncrewed-vessel work. Beyond Ghost Shark and small uncrewed surface vessel trials, no consolidated public RAN uncrewed inventory or fleet plan is loaded.
systems_rows.notes: Captured here only as published context. Loaded as partial because no single public source lists every RAN uncrewed maritime program with a consistent definition.

program_rows.program_name: 2024 Integrated Investment Program - uncrewed and autonomous lines
program_rows.headline: The 2024 IIP describes investment lines that include uncrewed aerial, surface, undersea and counter-drone capabilities, but it does not publish a single clean drone-only budget figure.
program_rows.notes: Treat as plan context only. The IIP groups uncrewed systems with broader capability lines; there is no source-safe drone-only line item to extract.

program_rows.headline: The 2026 NDS reaffirms the broader capability investment envelope (around A$425b in capability investment over the decade to 2035-36 per the official factsheet) but it does not separate a drone-only or uncrewed-only budget within that envelope.
program_rows.notes: Funding envelope context only. Do not present this number as drone funding.

program_rows.program_name: Defence Industry Development Strategy 2024 - autonomous systems
program_rows.headline: The 2024 Defence Industry Development Strategy lists autonomous systems among priority sovereign capabilities. It frames industrial intent rather than a procurement budget or fleet count.
program_rows.notes: Strategy context only. Not a budget or readiness statement.

funding_rows.boundary_note: No public Defence budget or factsheet publishes a clean drone-only or uncrewed-only line. The 2024 IIP and 2026 NDS reference uncrewed and autonomous capabilities inside broader investment envelopes, but those envelopes also include crewed platforms, sustainment, infrastructure and workforce. Presenting a bundled IIP or NDS line as a drone budget would misrepresent the source.

Defence and Anduril Australia have publicly described the Ghost Shark XL-AUV as a co-funded sovereign development. The exact Commonwealth contribution is not published as a single, clean figure on the project page; treat as program context only.

takeaway_rows.title: Australia operates, acquires and is developing uncrewed systems across all domains
takeaway_rows.text: Public Defence project pages cover air (Triton, Ghost Bat), land (Integrator, counter-small UAS), and maritime (Ghost Shark) uncrewed work. The page keeps in-service, acquisition, development, sovereign program and counter-drone rows separate.

takeaway_rows.title: There is no clean public drone-only Defence budget
takeaway_rows.text: The 2024 Integrated Investment Program and the 2026 National Defence Strategy reference uncrewed and autonomous-system lines inside broader capability investment envelopes. Neither publishes a separate drone-only figure, so this page leaves that unavailable rather than guessing.

takeaway_rows.title: Counter-drone is its own line
takeaway_rows.text: LAND 156 c-sUAS is shown as a separate counter-drone capability row. It defends against adversary uncrewed systems and is not the same kind of line as the drones Australia operates or develops.

methodology_caveats: Quantities cite the named Defence project page rather than asserting a separately published operational fleet count.
Development programs (Ghost Bat, Ghost Shark) are not presented as fielded operational capability.
Counter-drone (LAND 156) is shown as a defensive line, not as an Australian uncrewed-platform count.
Sovereign programs (SDIP autonomous systems, DIDS) are industrial categories, not readiness or fleet counts.
No drone-only Defence budget is published here because no public Defence source separates one cleanly from the broader IIP / NDS envelopes.
No mission-capable, deployability, sortie-rate, basing, or live-availability data is loaded.
No classified or sensitive uncrewed systems are listed.
